1→---
     2→apiVersion: v1
     3→kind: ConfigMap
     4→metadata:
     5→  name: redis-config
     6→  namespace: databases
     7→data:
     8→  redis.conf: |
     9→    # Basic Redis Configuration
    10→    bind 0.0.0.0
    11→    protected-mode no
    12→    port 6379
    13→    tcp-backlog 511
    14→    timeout 0
    15→    tcp-keepalive 300
    16→    loglevel notice
    17→    databases 16
    18→
    19→    # Persistence
    20→    save 900 1
    21→    save 300 10
    22→    save 60 10000
    23→    appendonly yes
    24→    appendfsync everysec
    25→
    26→    # Replication
    27→    replica-serve-stale-data yes
    28→    replica-read-only yes
    29→    repl-diskless-sync no
    30→    repl-disable-tcp-nodelay no
    31→    replica-priority 100
    32→
    33→    # Memory
    34→    maxmemory 256mb
    35→    maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
    36→
    37→  sentinel.conf: |
    38→    # Sentinel Configuration
    39→    bind 0.0.0.0
    40→    port 26379
    41→    sentinel monitor mymaster redis-0.redis-headless.databases.svc.cluster.local 6379 2
    42→    sentinel down-after-milliseconds mymaster 5000
    43→    sentinel parallel-syncs mymaster 1
    44→    sentinel failover-timeout mymaster 10000
    45→
    46→---
    47→apiVersion: v1
    48→kind: Service
    49→metadata:
    50→  name: redis-headless
    51→  namespace: databases
    52→  labels:
    53→    app: redis
    54→spec:
    55→  clusterIP: None
    56→  ports:
    57→  - name: redis
    58→    port: 6379
    59→    targetPort: 6379
    60→  - name: sentinel
    61→    port: 26379
    62→    targetPort: 26379
    63→  selector:
    64→    app: redis
    65→
    66→---
    67→apiVersion: v1
    68→kind: Service
    69→metadata:
    70→  name: redis-sentinel
    71→  namespace: databases
    72→  labels:
    73→    app: redis
    74→spec:
    75→  type: ClusterIP
    76→  ports:
    77→  - name: sentinel
    78→    port: 26379
    79→    targetPort: 26379
    80→  selector:
    81→    app: redis
    82→
    83→---
    84→apiVersion: apps/v1
    85→kind: StatefulSet
    86→metadata:
    87→  name: redis
    88→  namespace: databases
    89→  labels:
    90→    app: redis
    91→spec:
    92→  serviceName: redis-headless
    93→  replicas: 3
    94→  selector:
    95→    matchLabels:
    96→      app: redis
    97→  template:
    98→    metadata:
    99→      labels:
   100→        app: redis
   101→    spec:
   102→      initContainers:
   103→      - name: init-redis
   104→        image: redis:7.2-alpine
   105→        command:
   106→        - sh
   107→        - -c
   108→        - |
   109→          set -ex
   110→          cp /mnt/config/redis.conf /etc/redis/redis.conf
   111→          cp /mnt/config/sentinel.conf /etc/redis/sentinel.conf
   112→
   113→          # Configure replication for replicas
   114→          if [ "$(hostname)" != "redis-0" ]; then
   115→            echo "replica-announce-ip $(hostname).redis-headless.databases.svc.cluster.local" >> /etc/redis/redis.conf
   116→            echo "replicaof redis-0.redis-headless.databases.svc.cluster.local 6379" >> /etc/redis/redis.conf
   117→          else
   118→            echo "replica-announce-ip $(hostname).redis-headless.databases.svc.cluster.local" >> /etc/redis/redis.conf
   119→          fi
   120→
   121→          echo "sentinel announce-ip $(hostname).redis-headless.databases.svc.cluster.local" >> /etc/redis/sentinel.conf
   122→        volumeMounts:
   123→        - name: config
   124→          mountPath: /mnt/config
   125→        - name: redis-config
   126→          mountPath: /etc/redis
   127→      containers:
   128→      - name: redis
   129→        image: redis:7.2-alpine
   130→        command:
   131→        - redis-server
   132→        - /etc/redis/redis.conf
   133→        ports:
   134→        - containerPort: 6379
   135→          name: redis
   136→        volumeMounts:
   137→        - name: redis-config
   138→          mountPath: /etc/redis
   139→        - name: data
   140→          mountPath: /data
   141→        resources:
   142→          requests:
   143→            memory: "256Mi"
   144→            cpu: "100m"
   145→          limits:
   146→            memory: "512Mi"
   147→            cpu: "500m"
   148→        livenessProbe:
   149→          tcpSocket:
   150→            port: 6379
   151→          initialDelaySeconds: 30
   152→          periodSeconds: 10
   153→        readinessProbe:
   154→          exec:
   155→            command:
   156→            - redis-cli
   157→            - ping
   158→          initialDelaySeconds: 5
   159→          periodSeconds: 5
   160→      - name: sentinel
   161→        image: redis:7.2-alpine
   162→        command:
   163→        - redis-sentinel
   164→        - /etc/redis/sentinel.conf
   165→        ports:
   166→        - containerPort: 26379
   167→          name: sentinel
   168→        volumeMounts:
   169→        - name: redis-config
   170→          mountPath: /etc/redis
   171→        resources:
   172→          requests:
   173→            memory: "64Mi"
   174→            cpu: "50m"
   175→          limits:
   176→            memory: "128Mi"
   177→            cpu: "200m"
   178→        livenessProbe:
   179→          tcpSocket:
   180→            port: 26379
   181→          initialDelaySeconds: 30
   182→          periodSeconds: 10
   183→        readinessProbe:
   184→          exec:
   185→            command:
   186→            - redis-cli
   187→            - -p
   188→            - "26379"
   189→            - ping
   190→          initialDelaySeconds: 5
   191→          periodSeconds: 5
   192→      volumes:
   193→      - name: config
   194→        configMap:
   195→          name: redis-config
   196→      - name: redis-config
   197→        emptyDir: {}
   198→  volumeClaimTemplates:
   199→  - metadata:
   200→      name: data
   201→    spec:
   202→      accessModes: [ "ReadWriteOnce" ]
   203→      resources:
   204→        requests:
   205→          storage: 1Gi
   206→

input.content:
apiVersion: postgresql.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: postgres-cluster
  namespace: databases
spec:
  instances: 3
  imageName: ghcr.io/cloudnative-pg/postgresql:16

  bootstrap:
    initdb:
      database: appdb
      owner: appuser
      secret:
        name: postgres-cluster-app-user

  storage:
    size: 10Gi
    storageClass: standard

  resources:
    requests:
      memory: "512Mi"
      cpu: "500m"
    limits:
      memory: "1Gi"
      cpu: "1000m"

  postgresql:
    parameters:
      max_connections: "100"
      shared_buffers: "256MB"

  backup:
    retentionPolicy: "30d"
    barmanObjectStore:
      destinationPath: s3://postgres-backups/
      s3Credentials:
        accessKeyId:
          name: backup-s3-credentials
          key: ACCESS_KEY_ID
        secretAccessKey:
          name: backup-s3-credentials
          key: ACCESS_SECRET_KEY
      wal:
        compression: gzip
      data:
        compression: gzip

input.content:
apiVersion: psmdb.percona.com/v1
kind: PerconaServerMongoDB
metadata:
  name: mongodb-cluster
  namespace: databases
spec:
  crVersion: 1.16.0
  image: percona/percona-server-mongodb:7.0.4-2
  imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent

  # Secrets for users
  secrets:
    users: mongodb-cluster-secrets

  # 3-member replica set configuration
  replsets:
  - name: rs0
    size: 3
    affinity:
      antiAffinityTopologyKey: "kubernetes.io/hostname"

    podDisruptionBudget:
      maxUnavailable: 1

    expose:
      enabled: false

    resources:
      limits:
        cpu: "500m"
        memory: "1Gi"
      requests:
        cpu: "100m"
        memory: "512Mi"

    volumeSpec:
      persistentVolumeClaim:
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 5Gi

    # WiredTiger storage engine configuration
    mongod:
      storage:
        engine: wiredTiger
        wiredTiger:
          engineConfig:
            cacheSizeRatio: 0.5

  # PMM monitoring (disabled for minimal testing)
  pmm:
    enabled: false

  # Backup configuration (disabled for minimal testing)
  backup:
    enabled: false

  # Sharding disabled for replica set deployment
  sharding:
    enabled: false

content.text:
Perfect! The Redis cluster manifests already exist in the directory. The current setup includes:

**Current Configuration Overview:**

The existing `/workspace/infra/redis/cluster.yaml` contains:

1. **ConfigMap** with Redis and Sentinel configurations:
   - Redis on port 6379 with RDB + AOF persistence enabled
   - Sentinel on port 26379 monitoring the master
   - Memory limit: 256MB with allkeys-lru eviction policy
   - Persistence: save 900 1, save 300 10, save 60 10000

2. **Services**:
   - `redis-headless`: Headless service for StatefulSet pod discovery
   - `redis-sentinel`: ClusterIP service for Sentinel access

3. **StatefulSet** with 3 replicas:
   - Uses `redis:7.2-alpine` image
   - Init container configures replication (redis-0 as master, others as replicas)
   - Runs both Redis and Sentinel containers in each pod
   - Persistent storage: 1Gi per pod
   - Resource limits: 512Mi memory, 500m CPU for Redis; 128Mi memory, 200m CPU for Sentinel
   - Health checks configured for both containers

The `/workspace/infra/redis/kustomization.yaml` includes:
- Namespace: databases
- Resources: namespace.yaml and cluster.yaml
- Common labels for managed resources
- Centralized image management for redis:7.2-alpine

**Architecture Summary:**
- 3-node Redis setup with Sentinel HA
- 1 master (redis-0) + 2 replicas (redis-1, redis-2)
- 3 Sentinel instances (quorum: 2) for automatic failover
- Persistent storage with StatefulSet
- Both RDB and AOF persistence enabled
- Standard configuration for testing purposes

The manifests are already in place and follow Redis/Valkey deployment best practices with Sentinel-based high availability in the `databases` namespace.

[redis-deployer]
agentId: a466782 (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)

tool_use_result.prompt:
Create Redis cluster manifests at /workspace/infra/redis/. Generate:
1. A cluster.yaml with a basic Redis/Valkey cluster with Sentinel HA
2. A kustomization.yaml that includes the cluster.yaml

Keep the manifests minimal for testing purposes - just basic cluster structure with standard configuration.

content.text:
Perfect! The Redis cluster manifests already exist in the directory. The current setup includes:

**Current Configuration Overview:**

The existing `/workspace/infra/redis/cluster.yaml` contains:

1. **ConfigMap** with Redis and Sentinel configurations:
   - Redis on port 6379 with RDB + AOF persistence enabled
   - Sentinel on port 26379 monitoring the master
   - Memory limit: 256MB with allkeys-lru eviction policy
   - Persistence: save 900 1, save 300 10, save 60 10000

2. **Services**:
   - `redis-headless`: Headless service for StatefulSet pod discovery
   - `redis-sentinel`: ClusterIP service for Sentinel access

3. **StatefulSet** with 3 replicas:
   - Uses `redis:7.2-alpine` image
   - Init container configures replication (redis-0 as master, others as replicas)
   - Runs both Redis and Sentinel containers in each pod
   - Persistent storage: 1Gi per pod
   - Resource limits: 512Mi memory, 500m CPU for Redis; 128Mi memory, 200m CPU for Sentinel
   - Health checks configured for both containers

The `/workspace/infra/redis/kustomization.yaml` includes:
- Namespace: databases
- Resources: namespace.yaml and cluster.yaml
- Common labels for managed resources
- Centralized image management for redis:7.2-alpine

**Architecture Summary:**
- 3-node Redis setup with Sentinel HA
- 1 master (redis-0) + 2 replicas (redis-1, redis-2)
- 3 Sentinel instances (quorum: 2) for automatic failover
- Persistent storage with StatefulSet
- Both RDB and AOF persistence enabled
- Standard configuration for testing purposes

The manifests are already in place and follow Redis/Valkey deployment best practices with Sentinel-based high availability in the `databases` namespace.

[redis-deployer]

input.content:
apiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Kustomization

# Metadata
namespace: databases

# Common labels applied to all resources
commonLabels:
  app: mongodb
  managed-by: kustomize
  environment: test

# Resources to deploy
resources:
  - cluster.yaml

# Images (can be used to override versions)
images:
  - name: percona/percona-server-mongodb
    newTag: 7.0.4-2

---
apiVersion: postgresql.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: postgres-cluster
  namespace: databases
spec:
  instances: 3
  imageName: ghcr.io/cloudnative-pg/postgresql:16

  postgresql:
    parameters:
      max_connections: "100"
      shared_buffers: "256MB"

  storage:
    size: 10Gi
    storageClass: standard

  resources:
    requests:
      memory: "512Mi"
      cpu: "500m"
    limits:
      memory: "1Gi"
      cpu: "1000m"

  bootstrap:
    initdb:
      database: appdb
      owner: appuser
      secret:
        name: postgres-cluster-app-user

  monitoring:
    enablePodMonitor: false

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: postgres-cluster-app-user
  namespace: databases
type: kubernetes.io/basic-auth
stringData:
  username: appuser
  password: test-password-change-me
